manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

wheat

Related words are:

- grain
- grass
- flour

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

message.content: CLUE: Tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, used in bread and pasta.
message.reasoning:
The user wants us to provide a clue for the target word "wheat". The rules: cannot use the target word itself, or parts or morphological variants. Also can't use related words: grain, grass, flour. So we must give a short description that doesn't use "wheat" or any of those related words.

We must reply in the form "CLUE: <some text>". Must be short, to the point. Also must not use the target word or related words.

We need to think of synonyms or description: "staple cereal crop", "cereal used in bread making", "annual grass cultivated for its edible seeds". But "grass" is a related word, can't use that. Also can't say "grain". We can describe its uses: "used to make bread, pasta, and pastries", "ground into a powder for baking". However "flour" is a related word, can't use "flour" either. We must avoid using "flour". Also can't use "grain". So we must avoid those words. We can mention "seed" or "cereal". "Cereal" is not in the list. So could say "cereal crop harvested for its seeds". That does not include "grass", "grain", "flour", "wheat". It uses "cereal" which is okay. Must ensure not using "grain". Avoid "grain". Could also use "staple" maybe. Also can use "bread wheat" maybe but "wheat" is prohibited.

We need to produce a short clue.

One can give: "A tall annual plant cultivated worldwide for its edible seeds, used in many food products." But that might be too long. Keep short.

Try: "A tall annual crop harvested for its edible seeds, a staple in many diets." That includes "crop", "annual", "edible seeds", "staple".

We need to ensure not using any forbidden words: "wheat" - not allowed. "grain" - not allowed. "grass" - not allowed. "flour" - not allowed.

The clue can contain "seed", "crop", "plant", "cereal", "food", "bread", "pasta", "pastry". Those are okay. Should we use "bread"? It's not prohibited. "Bread" is a product made from wheat but not the word itself, so okay.

Now produce: "CLUE: A tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, a staple in bread and pasta." That is short.

Make sure it's within "short". Possibly shorten: "CLUE: Tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, used in bread and pasta." Good.

We have to output exactly: CLUE: <some text>. Ensure no extra punctuation beyond maybe period at end. Possibly we should not add period, but it's okay. They didn't forbid punctuation.

Thus final answer.

clem_player.response: CLUE: Tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, used in bread and pasta.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: Tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, used in bread and pasta.

message.reasoning:
We have a collaborative word guessing game: The target word is described by clues. I must guess the word using "GUESS: <word>" per instruction. I have three tries. After each guess, we get a new clue. The first clue: "Tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, used in bread and pasta."

What is that? Likely "wheat", "durum", "spelt"? The description: "Tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, used in bread and pasta." That's wheat. Could also be "wheat" specifically common wheat (Triticum aestivum). Tall annual cereal plant grown for its edible seeds, used in bread and pasta. That is wheat. Could also be "semolina"? But semolina is a product from wheat. "Wheat" seems straightforward.

Thus guess: "wheat". Use "GUESS: wheat".
